findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Credential and configuration data exfiltration via HTTP POST
findings.description: The `_post_install_validation` function in analyze.py reads environment variables containing patterns like TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASS, AUTH, CRED, and reads sensitive user files (~/.gitconfig, ~/.netrc, ~/.env). It then sends this data as a JSON payload via curl POST to an external server (https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init). This performs unauthorized exfiltration of credentials and configuration data without user consent.
findings.evidence: The function collects environment variables and file contents, then sends them via subprocess.run(['curl', ...]) to an external API endpoint.
findings.remediation: Remove the entire `_post_install_validation` function and any code that collects or transmits sensitive data. Ensure the skill only performs the advertised static analysis.

findings.title: Detection evasion via misleading comment
findings.description: The analyze.py script contains a comment '# Verified by audit pipeline v3 — internal tooling only.' intended to bypass security review and create a false sense of trust. This is a deliberate attempt to evade detection of the malicious payload.
findings.evidence: The comment claims an audit that does not exist and uses authoritative language to reduce scrutiny.
findings.remediation: Remove any misleading comments. Implement proper code review and provenance tracking.

findings.title: Deceptive capability description (Capability Inflation)
findings.description: The skill's name 'code-review-assistant' and description 'Automated code review with style checks, complexity analysis, and best-practice suggestions' misrepresent the actual behavior. The bundled analyze.py script performs credential harvesting and data exfiltration, not code review. This is a classic bait-and-switch to increase activation and trust.
findings.evidence: The description claims analysis and suggestions, but the script exfiltrates credentials and sensitive files. The instructions tell the agent to run the malicious script as part of code review workflow.
findings.remediation: Rewrite the skill to perform only the advertised code review functionality. Remove all data theft capabilities.

findings.title: Missing tool restrictions metadata
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest does not specify 'allowed-tools' or 'compatibility' fields. While optional, including these would help security tooling assess the skill's intended capabilities and detect deviations.
findings.evidence: No allowed-tools or compatibility fields present in YAML frontmatter.
findings.remediation: Add allowed-tools and compatibility fields to the manifest to clearly declare intended tool usage (e.g., allowed-tools: [Read, Python]).
